solutions.title: Tutorial
solutions.content:
1211D - Teams
Consider that $$$a  \lt  b$$$. If you have a chain of ratings $$$rr_1, rr_2, \dots, rr_t$$$ such that $$$rr_{i+1} = rr_i \cdot k$$$ for each $$$1 \le i  \lt  t$$$ and $$$cc_i$$$ is number of players with the rating equals $$$rr_i$$$, then you can compose teams in a greedy way from left to right. This is true, since if you move a composed team along the chain to the left, then only improve the situation.
In the opposite case $$$a \ge b$$$ you should compose teams greedy from right to left.
So for each rating compute the number of players in a map (say, $$$rm$$$). After it iterate through all the keys (possible ratings) in increasing order if $$$a \lt b$$$ or in decreasing order if $$$a \ge b$$$. Each time try compose as many teams as you can using two ratings $$$r$$$ and $$$r \cdot k$$$ (i.e. increase the answer by $$$\min(rm[r] / a, rm[r \cdot k] / b)$$$).
Here is the example of a solution in Kotlin:
import kotlin.math.*
fun main() {
val (n, a, b, k) = readLine()!!.split(" ").map { it.toInt() }
val rm = readLine()!!.split(" ").map { it.toInt() }
.groupingBy { it }.eachCount()
.mapValues { Left(it.value) }
val rl = rm.keys.sorted()
.let { if (a >= b) it else it.reversed() }
var ans = 0
for (r in rl) {
val p = rm.getValue(r)
if (p.cnt < a) continue
val q = rm[r * k] ?: continue
if (q.cnt < b) continue
val t = min(p.cnt / a, q.cnt / b)
ans += t
p.cnt -= a * t
q.cnt -= b * t
}
println(ans)
}
data class Left(var cnt: Int)
